what: Activate communication within Swimmer Plot module
title: Communication with DaVinci modules
text: default, receiver_id parameter mod_swimmerplot() call module list definition set NULL, means communication functionality disabled. enable switching another DaVinci module, set receiver_id module ID counterpart module.

title: Communication with DaVinci modules
text: Example code module list definition turn communication feature Swimmer Plot Patient Profile module: information module communication DaVinci apps, see dv.manager documentation. details configure Patient Profile module receive communication, see dv.papo documentation communication.
code: dm <- pharmaversesdtm::dm |>   dplyr::select(USUBJID, AGE, SEX, RACE, ARM, RFSTDTC, RFENDTC) |>    dplyr::filter(ARM != "Screen Failure")  ex <- dplyr::left_join(x = pharmaversesdtm::ex, y = dm, by = "USUBJID") |>   dplyr::mutate(     ex_ongoing = is.na(EXENDY),     ex_end = ifelse(ex_ongoing, EXSTDY + 10, EXENDY) # For ongoing treatments   )  rs <- dplyr::left_join(x = pharmaversesdtm::rs_onco, y = dm, by = "USUBJID") |>   dplyr::filter(RSTEST == "Overall Response") |>   dplyr::filter(RSEVAL == "INVESTIGATOR") |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(RSDY))  data_list <- list(dm = dm, ex = ex, rs = rs)  module_list <- list(   "Swimmer Plot" = dv.swimmerplot::mod_swimmerplot(     module_id = "swimmer1",     subject_level_dataset_name = "dm",     exposure_dataset_name = "ex",     response_dataset_name = "rs",     subjid_var = "USUBJID",     group_by_vars = c("SEX", "RACE", "ARM"),     sort_by_vars = c("AGE", "USUBJID"),     sort_direction = "asc",     trt_start_day_var = "EXSTDY",     trt_end_day_var = "ex_end",     trt_ongoing_var = "ex_ongoing",     trt_tooltip_vars = c(       "Subject ID: " = "USUBJID",       "Exposure: " = "EXTRT",       "Start Day: " = "EXSTDY",       "End Day: " = "EXENDY"     ),     trt_group_var = "EXTRT",     trt_legend_label = "Treatment",     result_study_day_var = "RSDY",     result_tooltip_vars = c(       "Subject ID: " = "USUBJID",       "Study Day: " = "RSDY",       "Response: " = "RSORRES"     ),     result_cat_var = "RSORRES",     result_legend_label = "Response",     plot_title = "Subject Treatment and Response",     plot_x_label = "Study Day",     plot_y_label = "Subject ID",     plot_width = 10,     receiver_id = "papo1"   ),   "Patient Profile" = dv.papo::mod_patient_profile(     module_id = "papo1",     subject_level_dataset_name = "dm",     subjid_var = "USUBJID",     summary = list(       vars = c("USUBJID", "AGE", "SEX", "RACE", "ARM"),       column_count = 3L     ),     listings = list(       "Exposure" = list(         dataset = "ex",         default_vars = c("EXSTDY", "EXENDY", "EXTRT", "EXDOSE", "EXDOSU")       ),       "Response" = list(         dataset = "rs",         default_vars = c("RSDY", "RSORRES", "RSTEST")       )     ),     sender_ids = "swimmer1"   ) )  dv.manager::run_app(   data = list("Study Data" = data_list),   module_list = module_list,   filter_data = "dm",   filter_key = "USUBJID" )

title: Swimmer Plot Module
text: Swimmer plots specialized visualizations display subject-level data time. subject represented horizontal “swim lane” showing treatment exposures optionally clinical outcomes responses. vignette demonstrates prepare data use swimmer plot module within DaVinci application. Note: Response data completely optional. can create swimmer plots show exposure data, include exposure response data depending analysis needs.

title: Swimmer Plot Module
text: swimmer plot module requires minimum two types datasets, third optional: Subject Level Dataset (Required): Contains subject level demographic information (e.g., dm, adsl) Exposure Dataset (Required): Contains treatment exposure information (e.g., ex, adex) Response Dataset (Optional): Contains outcome response assessments (e.g., rs, adrs, clinical outcomes) example, ’ll use SDTM data pharmaversesdtm package, contains oncology data, module can adapted various therapeutic areas. ’ll prepare three datasets, remember response dataset optional:
code: dm <- pharmaversesdtm::dm |>   dplyr::select(USUBJID, AGE, SEX, RACE, ARM, RFSTDTC, RFENDTC) |>    dplyr::filter(ARM != "Screen Failure")  ex <- dplyr::left_join(x = pharmaversesdtm::ex, y = dm, by = "USUBJID") |>   dplyr::mutate(     study_day = as.numeric(as.Date(RFENDTC) - as.Date(RFSTDTC)),     ex_trt = paste(EXTRT, EXDOSE, EXDOSU),           ex_ongoing = is.na(EXENDY),     ex_end = ifelse(ex_ongoing, EXSTDY + 10, EXENDY) # For ongoing treatments   )  # Optional: Response dataset for clinical outcomes # This example uses oncology response data, but any type of response or outcome data can be used # You can omit this entirely if you only want to show exposure data rs <- dplyr::left_join(x = pharmaversesdtm::rs_onco, y = dm, by = "USUBJID") |>   dplyr::filter(RSTEST == "Overall Response") |>   dplyr::filter(RSEVAL == "INVESTIGATOR") |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(RSDY))  sdtm_datasets <- list(dm = dm, ex = ex, rs = rs)

what: Creating a Swimmer Plot Without Response Data (Exposure Only)
title: Swimmer Plot Module
text: can create swimmer plot displays subject-level exposure data setting response_dataset_name = NULL. useful want focus solely treatment patterns response data available: Important: response_dataset_name set NULL, response-related parameters (result_study_day_var, result_tooltip_vars, result_cat_var, result_legend_label, shape_mapping) can omitted used. plot show treatment exposure timelines.
code: swimmer_plot_exposure_only <- dv.swimmerplot::mod_swimmerplot(     module_id = "mod1",     subject_level_dataset_name = "dm",     exposure_dataset_name = "ex",     response_dataset_name = NULL,  # Omit response dataset     subjid_var = "USUBJID",     group_by_vars = c("SEX", "ARM"),     sort_by_vars = c("AGE", "USUBJID"),     sort_direction = "asc",     trt_start_day_var = "EXSTDY",     trt_end_day_var = "ex_end",     trt_group_var = "ex_trt",     trt_ongoing_var = "ex_ongoing",     trt_tooltip_vars = c(       "Subject ID: " = "USUBJID",       "Exposure: " = "ex_trt",       "Start Day: " = "EXSTDY",       "End Day: " = "EXENDY"     ),     trt_legend_label = "Exposure",     color_palette = c(       "PLACEBO 0 mg" = "#FFCCBC",       "XANOMELINE 54 mg" = "#B3E5FC",       "XANOMELINE 81 mg" = "#C8E6C9"     ),     plot_title = "Subject Exposure Only",     plot_subtitle = "Arrows indicate ongoing exposure with missing end times",     plot_x_label = "Study Day",     plot_y_label = "Subject ID",     plot_width = 10,     plot_height = NULL )

what: Creating a Swimmer Plot Module with Response Data (Full Featured)
title: Swimmer Plot Module
text: comprehensive visualization, can include exposure response data. example shows use mod_swimmerplot function available features, including response assessments displayed points timeline:
code: swimmer_plot_module <- dv.swimmerplot::mod_swimmerplot(     module_id = "mod2",     subject_level_dataset_name = "dm",     exposure_dataset_name = "ex",     response_dataset_name = "rs",     subjid_var = "USUBJID",     group_by_vars = c("SEX", "RACE"),     sort_by_vars = c("AGE", "USUBJID"),     sort_direction = "asc",     trt_start_day_var = "EXSTDY",     trt_end_day_var = "ex_end",     trt_group_var = "ex_trt",     trt_ongoing_var = "ex_ongoing",     trt_tooltip_vars = c(       "Subject ID: " = "USUBJID",       "Exposure: " = "ex_trt",       "Start Day: " = "EXSTDY",       "End Day: " = "EXENDY"     ),     result_study_day_var = "RSDY",     result_tooltip_vars = c(       "Subject ID: " = "USUBJID",       "Study Day: " = "RSDY",       "Response: " = "RSORRES"     ),     result_cat_var = "RSORRES",     trt_legend_label = "Exposure",     result_legend_label = "Response",     color_palette = c(       "PLACEBO 0 mg" = "#FFCCBC",       "XANOMELINE 54 mg" = "#B3E5FC",       "XANOMELINE 81 mg" = "#C8E6C9"     ),     shape_mapping = c(       "CR" = 16,  # filled circle - Complete Response       "PR" = 17,  # filled triangle - Partial Response       "SD" = 15,  # filled diamond - Stable Disease       "PD" = 18   # filled square - Progressive Disease     ),     plot_title = "Interactive Swimmer Plot of Subject-Level Exposure and Response Data",     plot_subtitle = "Arrows indicate ongoing exposure with missing end times",     plot_x_label = "Study Day",     plot_y_label = "Subject ID",     plot_width = 10,     plot_height = NULL )

title: Swimmer Plot Module
text: Finally, ’ll use run_app function dv.manager package create Shiny application includes swimmer plot modules - one showing exposure one showing exposure response data:
code: dv.manager::run_app(   data = list("SDTM Datasets" = sdtm_datasets),   module_list = list(     "Swimmer Plot - Exposure Only" = swimmer_plot_exposure_only,     "Swimmer Plot with Response" = swimmer_plot_module   ),   title = "Swimmer Plot Examples",   filter_data = "dm",   filter_key = "USUBJID" )

Swimmer Plot Module — mod_swimmerplot
text: `mod_swimmerplot` DaVinci Shiny module displays subject-level treatment exposures outcomes time. consists interactive visualization following features: subject represented horizontal "swim lane" Treatment exposures shown horizontal bars along timeline Clinical outcomes responses shown points along timeline (optional) Subjects can grouped categorical variables Interactive tooltips provide additional information hover Optional linking modules (e.g., Patient Profile) clicking subjects,  treatment duration bars, response points

title: Swimmer Plot Module — mod_swimmerplot
code: mod_swimmerplot(   module_id,   subject_level_dataset_name = "dm",   exposure_dataset_name = "ex",   response_dataset_name = NULL,   subjid_var = "USUBJID",   group_by_vars = c("SEX", "RACE"),   sort_by_vars = NULL,   sort_direction = "asc",   trt_start_day_var = "EXSTDY",   trt_end_day_var = "EXENDY",   trt_ongoing_var = NULL,   trt_tooltip_vars = NULL,   trt_group_var = "EXTRT",   trt_legend_label = NULL,   color_palette = NULL,   result_study_day_var = NULL,   result_tooltip_vars = NULL,   result_cat_var = NULL,   result_legend_label = NULL,   shape_mapping = NULL,   plot_title = NULL,   plot_subtitle = NULL,   plot_x_label = NULL,   plot_y_label = NULL,   plot_width = NULL,   plot_height = NULL,   receiver_id = NULL )

title: Swimmer Plot Module — mod_swimmerplot
text: module_id `[character(1)]` Unique ID module. subject_level_dataset_name `[character(1)]` Name dataset containing subject-level dataset. exposure_dataset_name `[character(1)]` Name dataset containing exposure data. response_dataset_name `[character(1)]` Name dataset containing response data. Set NULL  create plot without response data. subjid_var `[character(1)]` Name variable representing subject ID. group_by_vars `[character(n)]` Name variables group subjects plot. sort_by_vars `[character(n)]` Name variables sort plot. sort_direction `[character(1)]` Direction sort ("asc" ascending, "desc" descending). trt_start_day_var `[character(1)]` Name variable representing start exposure. trt_end_day_var `[character(1)]` Name variable representing end exposure. trt_ongoing_var `[character(1)]` Name variable indicating ongoing exposure. trt_tooltip_vars `[character(n)]` Character vector variables tooltip information exposure. Optionally named add labels tooltips (e.g., c("Dose: " = "EXDOSE")). trt_group_var `[character(1)]` Name variable representing type exposure. trt_legend_label `[character(1)]` Legend title exposure type. color_palette `[character(n)]` Named character vector mapping exposure types colors  (e.g., c("Drug " = "#FF0000", "Drug B" = "#00FF00")). result_study_day_var `[character(1)]` Name variable representing response time.  used response_dataset_name provided. result_tooltip_vars `[character(n)]` Character vector variables tooltip information response. Optionally named add labels tooltips (e.g., c("Response: " = "RSORRES")).  used response_dataset_name provided. result_cat_var `[character(1)]` Name variable representing type response.  used response_dataset_name provided. result_legend_label `[character(1)]` Legend title response type.  used response_dataset_name provided. shape_mapping `[numeric(n)]` Named numeric vector mapping response types point shapes  (e.g., c("CR" = 16, "PR" = 17)). used response_dataset_name provided. plot_title `[character(1)]` Title plot. plot_subtitle `[character(1)]` Subtitle plot. plot_x_label `[character(1)]` Label x-axis. plot_y_label `[character(1)]` Label y-axis. plot_width `[numeric(1)]` Width plot inches. plot_height `[numeric(1)]` Height plot inches.  NULL, height calculated automatically based number subjects. receiver_id `[character(1)]` ID module communicate (e.g., Patient Profile module ID).  Set NULL disable communication functionality.

title: Swimmer Plot Module — mod_swimmerplot
text: list composed following elements: `ui`: Shiny UI function. `server`: Shiny server function. `module_id`: Shiny unique identifier.

text: ... Named parameters collected constants

title: Build a collection of named constants — pack_of_constants
text: Shiny uses strings IDs link UI server elements. E.g:   foo_UI(id = ns("foo")) ...   foo_server(id = "foo") pattern makes easy programmers fall trap modifying one instance string literal "foo" without modifying rest unaware problem bug hit. also easy mistakes uses "foo" identifier uses (text labels, ...) , often case, parameter explicitly named. One easy fix consists using global variables instead plain string literals. case previous example, mean:   ID_FOO <- "foo"   foo_UI(ns(ID_FOO)) ...   foo_server(ID_FOO) simple addition makes purpose ID_FOO clear also fails gracefully ID_FOO instances updated synchronously along codebase. drawback polluting global namespace identifier variables. easily solved creating container constants, purpose pack_of_constants alias.  ID <- pack_of_constants(FOO = "foo", BAR = "bar")  ID$FOO  "foo"  ID$BA  Error `$.pack_of_constants`(ID, BA) :  Pack constants "ID" contain "BA" pack constants plain named list enforces elements unique, non-null names. tagged S3 object override extraction operators. use checkmate unnecessary, Good Library(TM) module rely anyways

what: Create an interactive swimmer plot — swimmerplot
title: Create an interactive swimmer plot — swimmerplot
text: Generates interactive swimmer plot showing subject-level treatment exposure periods response events. Response data optional - provided, plot show subject-level exposure data.

title: Create an interactive swimmer plot — swimmerplot
code: swimmerplot(   subject_level_dataset,   subjid_var,   group_by_vars = NULL,   sort_by_vars = NULL,   sort_direction = "asc",   exposure_dataset,   trt_start_day_var,   trt_end_day_var,   trt_ongoing_var,   trt_tooltip_vars = NULL,   trt_group_var = NULL,   trt_legend_label = "Treatment",   color_palette = NULL,   response_dataset = NULL,   result_study_day_var = NULL,   result_tooltip_vars = NULL,   result_cat_var = NULL,   result_legend_label = "Response",   shape_mapping = NULL,   plot_title = NULL,   plot_subtitle = NULL,   plot_x_label = "Study Day",   plot_y_label = "Subject ID",   plot_width = NULL,   plot_height = NULL,   enable_jumping = FALSE,   interactive_plot = TRUE )

title: Create an interactive swimmer plot — swimmerplot
text: subject_level_dataset `[data.frame]` Dataset containing subject-level data. subjid_var `[character(1)]` Name variable representing subject ID. group_by_vars `[character(n)]` Name variables group subjects plot. sort_by_vars `[character(n)]` Name variables sort plot. sort_direction `[character(1)]` Direction sort ("asc" ascending, "desc" descending). exposure_dataset `[data.frame]` Dataset containing treatment exposure data. trt_start_day_var `[character(1)]` Name variable representing start exposure. trt_end_day_var `[character(1)]` Name variable representing end exposure. trt_ongoing_var `[character(1)]` Name variable indicating ongoing exposure. trt_tooltip_vars `[character(n)]` Character vector variables tooltip information exposure.  Optionally named add labels tooltips (e.g., c("Dose: " = "EXDOSE")). trt_group_var `[character(1)]` Name variable representing type exposure. trt_legend_label `[character(1)]` Legend title exposure type. color_palette `[character(n)]` Named character vector mapping exposure types colors  (e.g., c("Drug " = "#FF0000", "Drug B" = "#00FF00")). response_dataset `[data.frame]` Dataset containing response/event data.  Optional - set NULL create plot without response data. result_study_day_var `[character(1)]` Name variable representing response time.  used response_dataset provided. result_tooltip_vars `[character(n)]` Character vector variables tooltip information response. Optionally named add labels tooltips (e.g., c("Response: " = "RSORRES")).  used response_dataset provided. result_cat_var `[character(1)]` Name variable representing type response.  used response_dataset provided. result_legend_label `[character(1)]` Legend title response type.  used response_dataset provided. shape_mapping `[numeric(n)]` Named numeric vector mapping response types point shapes  (e.g., c("CR" = 16, "PR" = 17)). used response_dataset provided. plot_title `[character(1)]` Title plot. plot_subtitle `[character(1)]` Subtitle plot. plot_x_label `[character(1)]` Label x-axis. plot_y_label `[character(1)]` Label y-axis. plot_width `[numeric(1)]` Width graphics region inches. plot_height `[numeric(1)]` Height graphics region inches. enable_jumping `[logical(1)]` Whether enable jumping behavior hover. interactive_plot `[logical(1)]` Whether return interactive girafe object (TRUE)  ggplot2 object (FALSE).

title: Create an interactive swimmer plot — swimmerplot
text: ggiraph interactive plot object interactive_plot=TRUE, otherwise ggplot2 object

title: Swimmer Plot Module UI — swimmerplot_UI

code: swimmerplot_UI(   id,   group_by_vars = NULL,   sort_by_vars = NULL,   jumping_enabled = FALSE )

title: Swimmer Plot Module UI — swimmerplot_UI

Must match ID provided [swimmerplot_server()]. group_by_vars `[character(n)]` Variables available grouping subjects. sort_by_vars `[character(n)]` Variables available sorting subjects. jumping_enabled `[logical(1)]` Whether clicking subject enable navigation detail view.

title: Swimmer Plot Module UI — swimmerplot_UI
text: Shiny UI.

title: Swimmer Plot Module server — swimmerplot_server
text: (use outside DaVinci framework) Runs server populates UI returned [swimmerplot_UI()]. Requires matching call function.

title: Swimmer Plot Module server — swimmerplot_server
code: swimmerplot_server(   id,   subject_level_dataset,   exposure_dataset,   response_dataset,   subjid_var,   trt_start_day_var,   trt_end_day_var,   trt_ongoing_var,   trt_tooltip_vars = NULL,   trt_group_var = NULL,   trt_legend_label = "Treatment",   color_palette = NULL,   result_study_day_var = NULL,   result_tooltip_vars = NULL,   result_cat_var = NULL,   result_legend_label = "Response",   shape_mapping = NULL,   plot_title = NULL,   plot_subtitle = NULL,   plot_x_label = "Study Day",   plot_y_label = "Subject ID",   plot_width = NULL,   plot_height = NULL,   sort_by_vars = NULL,   sort_direction = "asc",   receiver_id = NULL,   afmm = NULL )

title: Swimmer Plot Module server — swimmerplot_server
text: id `[character(1)]` Unique shiny ID. Must match ID provided [swimmerplot_server()]. subject_level_dataset `[reactive(data.frame)]` Subject-level dataset containing baseline information. exposure_dataset `[reactive(data.frame)]` Dataset containing treatment exposure records. response_dataset `[reactive(data.frame)]` Dataset containing response/event records. Optional -  can reactive returns NULL create plot without response data. subjid_var `[character(1)]` Name variable representing subject ID. trt_start_day_var `[character(1)]` Name variable representing start exposure. trt_end_day_var `[character(1)]` Name variable representing end exposure. trt_ongoing_var `[character(1)]` Name variable indicating ongoing exposure. trt_tooltip_vars `[character(n)]` Character vector variables tooltip information exposure. Optionally named add labels tooltips (e.g., c("Dose: " = "EXDOSE")). trt_group_var `[character(1)]` Name variable representing type exposure. trt_legend_label `[character(1)]` Legend title exposure type. color_palette `[character(n)]` Named character vector mapping exposure types colors  (e.g., c("Drug " = "#FF0000", "Drug B" = "#00FF00")). result_study_day_var `[character(1)]` Name variable representing response time.  used response_dataset_name provided. result_tooltip_vars `[character(n)]` Character vector variables tooltip information response. Optionally named add labels tooltips (e.g., c("Response: " = "RSORRES")).  used response_dataset_name provided. result_cat_var `[character(1)]` Name variable representing type response.  used response_dataset_name provided. result_legend_label `[character(1)]` Legend title response type.  used response_dataset_name provided. shape_mapping `[numeric(n)]` Named numeric vector mapping response types point shapes  (e.g., c("CR" = 16, "PR" = 17)). used response_dataset_name provided. plot_title `[character(1)]` Title plot. plot_subtitle `[character(1)]` Subtitle plot. plot_x_label `[character(1)]` Label x-axis. plot_y_label `[character(1)]` Label y-axis. plot_width `[numeric(1)]` Width plot inches. plot_height `[numeric(1)]` Height plot inches.  NULL, height calculated automatically based number subjects. sort_by_vars `[character(n)]` Variables available sorting subjects. sort_direction `[character(1)]` Direction sort ("asc" ascending, "desc" descending). receiver_id `[character(1)]` ID module communicate (e.g., Patient Profile module ID).  Set NULL disable communication functionality. afmm `[list]` Arguments Module Manager containing datasets, utilities module communication channels.

title: dv.swimmerplot 1.1.0
text: Complete reimplementation visualization engine Migrated plotly ggplot2 ggiraph interactive visualization

title: dv.swimmerplot 1.0.0
text: First version dv.swimmerplot Interactive visualization using plotly
